citation.description: RemoteRef dataclass at line 38, TicketTransport at 58, OutboundMapper at 80, InboundMapper at 96, FieldSanitizer at 105, IdentityConvention at 122, SupportsLinks @runtime_checkable at 154, SupportsComments at 169, SupportsIncremental at 178, Backend facade at 194. test_backend_contract.py:16 imports Backend, FieldSanitizer, RemoteRef; test_backend_capability.py:14-18 imports all three capability Protocols. test_backend_contract.py:93-94 constructs RemoteRef(vendor=..., instance=..., remote_id=...).

criteria.criterion: _backend.py defines Backend, the five role Protocols, the three capability Protocols (SupportsLinks, SupportsComments, SupportsIncremental), and RemoteRef — importable via `from rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler._backend import Backend, RemoteRef`

citation.description: pytest.fixture(params=['fake','jira']) parameterizes the backend fixture over FakeBackend() and JiraBackend(transport=FakeTransport()), causing all contract tests to run against both. JiraBackend is defined in adapters/jira/backend.py:66 with vendor, transport, outbound, inbound, sanitizer, identity properties matching the Backend Protocol.

criteria.criterion: JiraBackend implements Backend; the contract suite passes when parameterized over both JiraBackend (injected mock transport) and FakeBackend — verified by `make test` running the suite twice via the `backend_factory` fixture

citation.description: test_outbound_map_is_byte_for_byte_jira_fields (literal dict expected), test_inbound_map_is_byte_for_byte_local_fields (literal dict expected), test_outbound_priority_value_map_pins_all_levels (pins all 5 priority levels), test_outbound_status_value_map_pins_all_states (pins all 6 status values), sanitizer tests with exact length and suffix checks. All use literal expected values.

criteria.criterion: Characterization tests assert the pure Jira mapper outputs are byte-for-byte identical to pre-story main for the representative fixtures — verified by literal expected values captured from current behavior

citation.description: The three new files exist exactly as specified: src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_backend.py, src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/backend.py, src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/identity.py. Ticket comment provides concrete attestation: commit 8f9d1c516 (Gerrit change 901, merged to main), with git diff --name-only showing only these three files plus test files. Core modules (applier.py, fetcher.py, etc.) contain select_backend() calls that the ticket attributes to later stories S3 (commit 245a086af) and S4 (commit eef1afa1f), not this story. This is corroborated by _backend_registry.py being a separate file (S3 scope per its own docstring).

criteria.criterion: No core differ/apply module is modified by this story — verified by git diff --name-only touching only _backend.py, adapters/jira/backend.py, adapters/jira/identity.py, and files under tests/

citation.description: test_outbound_priority_value_map_pins_all_levels asserts exact priority mappings {0:'Highest',1:'High',2:'Medium',3:'Low',4:'Lowest'} for every level. Dropping the priority map from _JiraOutbound.map_local_to_remote would cause this test to fail. test_outbound_map_is_byte_for_byte_jira_fields (line 25-42) also asserts priority:'High' for priority=1. The suite thus catches a delegation regression as required.

criteria.criterion: Teeth: perturbing a JiraBackend delegation (drop the priority map from OutboundMapper delegate) makes a contract/characterization test go RED, and restoring it returns to green

citation.description: Ticket comment attests: 'Verified+1 on Gerrit change 901 (MERGED) — CI ran the full suite + lint + typecheck on the patchset and passed; also run locally during implementation (contract+characterization+identity+capability suites 33/33, reconciler subtree 1333).' This is a concrete attestation naming the specific change ID (Gerrit 901), CI result (Verified+1), and observed test counts.
